title: MEA Recordings Process Schema
description: Comprehensive schema for multi-electrode array (MEA) recordings process, capturing experimental setup, acquisition parameters, environmental controls, and specialized techniques for intracellular recordings with enhanced biological and technical metadata

sampleType.description: Biological preparation characteristics, culture conditions, and maturation status

species.description: Species from which the biological preparation was derived (e.g., human, rat, mouse). Critical for physiological relevance and cross-species comparisons.

cellOrigin.description: Source of cells used in the preparation. Affects electrophysiological properties and experimental interpretation.

cellCultureType.description: Specific type of cardiomyocyte culture being used. Important for interpreting electrophysiological properties and drug responses. NRVMs represent primary ventricular cells, HL-1 is an immortalized atrial cell line, and hiPSC-CMs are stem cell-derived with potential ventricular or atrial characteristics.

sex.description: Sex of the biological source for the cells. Important for identifying potential sex-specific electrophysiological differences, though the research shows NRVMs from male and female sources exhibit similar electrophysiology.

biologicalPreparation.description: Specific biological preparation type (e.g., primary neurons, iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes, brain slices). Determines signal characteristics and analysis approaches. Must match electrode configuration and analysis pipeline.

cultureDimensionality.description: Culture dimensionality (2D monolayer or 3D structure). Affects network connectivity and signal propagation.

isogenicPair.description: Indicates whether the sample is part of an isogenic pair (genetically matched control)

surfaceCoating.description: Surface functionalization for cell adhesion (e.g., poly-D-lysine, laminin isoforms, fibronectin). Enhances cell attachment and recording stability. Specific laminin isoforms (LN-521, LN-221) can influence cell differentiation and electrophysiological properties. Fibronectin (40 μg/ml) is commonly used for cardiomyocyte cultures.

seedingMethod.description: Method used for seeding cells on the MEA. Droplet seeding over the central recording matrix significantly improves field potential detection capacity and amplitude compared to whole MEA seeding by keeping the internal reference electrode free of cell monolayer.

hydrophobicityTreatment.description: Pre-treatment of MEA to increase hydrophobicity for optimal droplet formation during seeding. Critical for establishing a confluent cardiomyocyte monolayer with droplet seeding methodology.

enabled.description: Whether hydrophobicity treatment was applied to the MEA

temperature.description: Temperature used for hydrophobicity treatment in degrees Celsius (°C)

duration.description: Duration of hydrophobicity treatment in hours

seedingDensity.description: Cell seeding density in cells per square centimeter (cells/cm²). Critical for network formation and activity patterns. For cardiomyocytes, typical densities range from 50,000-500,000 cells/cm². Droplet seeding achieves higher density (~1,000,000 cells/cm²) compared to whole MEA seeding (500,000 cells/cm²).

cellArrangement.description: Spatial arrangement of cells on the MEA. Affects signal quality and interpretation of network activity. Clustered arrangements may enhance local network connectivity while uniform arrangements provide broader coverage. hiPSC-CMs typically form clusters rather than confluent monolayers.

cellCultureMedia.description: Specific formulation of culture media used for cell maintenance. Different cell types require specialized media formulations that affect electrophysiological properties and drug responses.
examples: DMEM/Glutamax with specific supplements for NRVMs
Claycomb media with supplements for HL-1 cells
RPMI with B27 minus vitamin A for hiPSC-CMs

recordingMedium.description: Medium used during recording sessions. Affects cell viability and electrophysiological properties. May contain specific supplements for cardiac or neural applications. BPEL medium is commonly used for cardiomyocyte recordings.

daysInVitro.description: Culture age in days since plating or differentiation initiation. Critical for assessing cell maturation state and electrophysiological properties. For hiPSC-CMs, minimum 5-8 days typically required for regular spontaneous activity.

daysPostDifferentiation.description: For hiPSC-CMs, the number of days post-differentiation induction when cells were plated on MEA. Critical for electrophysiological maturity, with stable beating rates typically achieved at day 30 and reduced isoproterenol responsiveness at day 60.

maturationStage.description: Explicit maturation stage of the cells, critical for accurate drug response measurements. Immature cells may have different ion channel expression and drug responses compared to more mature cells. Maturation stage directly impacts conduction velocity measurements and action potential morphology.

electrode.description: Electrode physical, electrical, and configuration characteristics including specialized poration methods

material.description: Electrode material affecting impedance, biocompatibility, and signal-to-noise ratio. Common materials: platinum, gold, titanium nitride, iridium oxide, graphene. 3D fuzzy graphene (3DFG) enables intracellular recordings via optoporation.

coatingMaterial.description: Conductive polymer coating applied to electrode material to enhance signal amplitudes. PEDOT:PSS is commonly used for cardiomyocyte recordings to produce robust signal amplitudes.

shape.description: Geometric shape of individual electrodes. Affects electric field distribution and recording characteristics.

size.description: Electrode contact diameter or side length in micrometers (µm). Affects signal amplitude and single-unit resolution. For intracellular recordings, smaller electrodes (5-50 µm) are typically used.

spacing.description: Center-to-center distance between adjacent electrodes in micrometers (µm). Determines spatial resolution and crosstalk.

totalNumber.description: Total electrodes in the array. Defines coverage area and network analysis capability.

arrayLayout.description: Spatial arrangement pattern of the electrode array. Affects coverage and network analysis capabilities. Grid patterns are most common, but specialized layouts exist for specific applications.

arrayConfiguration.description: Specific configuration of the electrode array (e.g., 8x8 grid). Important for spatial analysis of conduction velocity and network activity patterns. Critical for accurate measurement of wave propagation across the tissue.

impedance.description: Electrode impedance at 1 kHz in ohms (Ω). Critical for signal amplitude and noise characteristics. Measured post-fabrication. For intracellular recordings, impedance values may change after poration. Standardized to Ω for consistency across measurements.

passivationMaterial.description: Material used for electrical passivation of electrode traces. Prevents electrical leakage and improves signal quality.

traceMaterial.description: Material used for electrode traces and contacts. Affects transparency and electrical properties.

referenceGroundConfiguration.description: Complete reference and ground electrode configuration

type.description: Type of reference electrode used in the recording setup.

position.description: Physical location of the reference electrode in the recording chamber. Keeping the internal reference electrode free of cell monolayer significantly improves field potential detection.

impedance.description: Impedance of reference electrode at 1 kHz in ohms (Ω).

type.description: Type of ground electrode used in the recording setup.

position.description: Physical location of the ground electrode in the recording chamber.

configuration.description: Relationship between reference and ground electrodes.

porationMethod.description: Method used for cell membrane poration to enable intracellular recordings. Optoporation with ultrafast pulsed laser is a novel technique for 3DFG electrodes.

optoporation.description: Parameters for laser-based optoporation technique used with 3DFG electrodes for intracellular recordings

enabled.description: Whether optoporation is used for intracellular recordings

laserWavelength.description: Wavelength of the laser used for optoporation in nanometers (nm). 1064 nm is commonly used for near-infrared applications.

pulseDuration.description: Duration of individual laser pulses in picoseconds (ps). Ultrafast pulses minimize thermal effects.

powerDensity.description: Laser power density at the electrode surface in watts per square millimeter (W/mm²). Critical for effective poration without cell damage.

spotSize.description: Diameter of the laser spot on the electrode surface in micrometers (µm). Determines localized poration area.

repetitionRate.description: Pulse repetition rate of the laser in hertz (Hz). Affects poration efficiency and thermal management.

totalDuration.description: Total duration of laser exposure for poration in milliseconds (ms).

acquisition.description: Data acquisition system configuration with explicit distinction between hardware and software processing stages

acquisitionHardware.description: Hardware system used for data acquisition. Required for reproducibility and system-specific considerations. Different systems have varying capabilities for electrode count, sampling rates, and signal processing.

samplingRate.description: Hardware sampling frequency per channel in Hertz (Hz). Must exceed Nyquist rate for target signals (e.g., >20 kHz for neural spikes, >10 kHz for cardiac field potentials). For intracellular recordings, adequate sampling is critical for capturing rapid AP phases. Cardiac recordings typically use 10 kHz sampling rate.

duration.description: Total recording duration in seconds. For continuous recordings, specify expected maximum duration. Cardiac recordings typically require longer durations (minutes to hours) to capture stable rhythm.

activeChannels.description: Number of electrodes actively recording. May be less than totalNumber in the array.

acquisitionSoftware.description: Software used for data acquisition. Required for reproducibility and analysis compatibility. Different software packages offer varying features for real-time analysis and data management.

gain.description: Amplification factor (dimensionless). Typical range 100-10,000 for extracellular signals. For intracellular recordings, gain requirements may differ due to higher signal amplitudes.

totalAmplification.description: Total amplification factor including all stages (dimensionless). Important for signal-to-noise calculations.

filtering.description: Complete filtering configuration with distinction between hardware and software stages

hardwareFiltering.description: Hardware-based filtering applied during signal acquisition

filterType.description: Type of hardware filter applied (bandpass, high-pass, low-pass, notch).

highPass.description: Hardware high-pass cutoff frequency in Hertz (Hz). Removes slow drifts (<300 Hz for spikes, >1 Hz for field potentials). For cardiac applications, typically set between 1-10 Hz.

lowPass.description: Hardware low-pass cutoff frequency in Hertz (Hz). Removes high-frequency noise (>3 kHz for spikes, <300 Hz for field potentials). For cardiac applications, typically set between 2-5 kHz.

notchFrequency.description: Notch filter frequency in Hertz (Hz) for removing specific interference (e.g., 50/60 Hz line noise).

filterType.description: Type of software filter applied (bandpass, high-pass, low-pass, notch).

highPass.description: Software high-pass cutoff frequency in Hertz (Hz).

lowPass.description: Software low-pass cutoff frequency in Hertz (Hz).

notchFrequency.description: Software notch filter frequency in Hertz (Hz).

environment.description: Environmental control parameters for physiological maintenance

temperature.description: Culture temperature in degrees Celsius (°C). Critical for cellular viability and electrophysiological properties. Cardiac applications typically require 37°C for physiological relevance.

co2.description: Carbon dioxide concentration in percentage (%) for pH buffering in bicarbonate-based media.

humidity.description: Relative humidity percentage (%) to prevent media evaporation during long-term recordings.

perfusion.description: Perfusion system parameters for continuous medium exchange during recordings. Important for maintaining physiological conditions during long-term experiments and drug application studies.

enabled.description: Whether active perfusion is used during recording

flowRate.description: Flow rate of perfusion medium in milliliters per minute (mL/min)

temperature.description: Temperature of perfusion medium in degrees Celsius (°C)

mediumComposition.description: Composition of perfusion medium, which may differ from standard culture medium

o2Concentration.description: Oxygen concentration percentage in the perfusion medium

co2Concentration.description: Carbon dioxide concentration percentage for pH buffering in the perfusion medium

oxygenation.description: Oxygen supply parameters for tissue viability

enabled.description: Whether active oxygenation is used during recording

o2Concentration.description: Oxygen concentration percentage in the perfusion medium

co2Concentration.description: Carbon dioxide concentration percentage for pH buffering in the perfusion medium

stimulation.description: Electrical, optical, or pharmacological stimulation parameters (optional for passive recordings)

enabled.description: Whether stimulation is active during recording

currentAmplitude.description: Electrical stimulation intensity in microamperes (µA) for current-controlled stimulation.

voltageAmplitude.description: Electrical stimulation intensity in millivolts (mV) for voltage-controlled stimulation.

pulseWidth.description: Duration of individual stimulation pulses in milliseconds (ms)

irradiance.description: Optical power density in milliwatts per square millimeter (mW/mm²) for light stimulation.

wavelength.description: Light wavelength in nanometers (nm) for optical stimulation. Critical for photoactive materials like P3HT and for optoporation techniques.

pulseWidth.description: Duration of individual stimulation pulses in milliseconds (ms)

drugName.description: Name of the pharmacological compound (for pharmacological stimulation)

treatmentType.description: Type of pharmacological treatment protocol. Critical for distinguishing between acute dose-response experiments and chronic treatment studies.

applicationMethod.description: Method of drug application to the recording chamber.

exposureDuration.description: Duration of drug exposure in minutes

stimulationProtocol.description: Pattern of stimulation delivery (e.g., single pulse, train, ramp). For pharmacological treatments, describes the detailed drug application schedule (e.g., 'aliquots added every 10 min' for acute dose-response or '24-h continuous exposure' for chronic treatment).

dataProcessing.description: Data processing parameters, software, and computational methods used for analysis

analysisSoftware.description: Software used for data analysis. Required for reproducibility. Different software packages offer specialized tools for cardiac or neural MEA analysis.

recordingType.description: Type of electrophysiological recording obtained. Critical for selecting appropriate analysis methods.

signalType.description: Primary signal type being analyzed. Determines appropriate analysis pipelines and metrics.

thresholdMethod.description: Method for determining spike detection threshold.

thresholdValue.description: Value used for spike detection threshold.

refractoryPeriod.description: Minimum time between detected spikes to avoid double-counting.

qtCorrectionMethod.description: Method used for QT interval correction. Important for cardiac applications where heart rate affects repolarization duration. Bazett's and Fridericia's formulas are commonly used but may not be appropriate for all hiPSC-CM lines as noted in research.

beatAnalysis.description: Parameters for analyzing individual beats in cardiac recordings

beatsForAnalysis.description: Number of consecutive beats used for averaging measurements. Typically 10 beats are used for stable measurements.

sitesForAnalysis.description: Number of electrode sites used for averaging measurements across the array. For NRVM and HL-1, typically 10 sites across the MEA.

outputMetrics.description: Target metrics to be extracted from recordings. Specifies analysis focus during acquisition setup. Field potential duration, corrected metrics, and action potential-related metrics (derived from field potential measurements) are particularly relevant for cardiac applications. Short-term variability (STV) is critical for assessing arrhythmia risk. Conduction velocity is essential for estimating sodium current effects.

metric.description: Name of the metric being extracted

unit.description: Unit of measurement for the metric

computationMethod.description: Description of how the metric is computed, including any specific algorithms or parameters used

referencePoint.description: Reference point used for timing measurements (e.g., 'maximum dV/dt', '50% repolarization')

validationCriteria.description: Criteria used to validate the metric extraction (e.g., 'SNR > 20 dB', 'consistent across 10 consecutive beats')

examples.computationMethod: Cross-correlation analysis with 1 ms time resolution
Time between peak negative deflection and peak of final positive deflection
Time between peak negative deflection and peak of final positive deflection
Relationship between spontaneous beating rate and field potential duration
Percentage change in spontaneous beating rate and field potential duration after 1 μM isoproterenol treatment
Distance between electrodes divided by time difference of activation peaks
examples.validationCriteria: Measured between electrodes at known distance
